sdo:itemDesc.dc:title: Handwritten letter from Oppel to Schenker, dated February 19, 1931
sdo:itemDesc.dc:description: In the latest in his series of lectures to a group of ladies, Oppel mentions Schenker by name for the first time. — A colleague of his, Robert Teichmüller, is considering preparing an edition of the Chopin études.

tei:opener.tei:salute: Mein lieber Herr Professor Schenker,Receipt of this letter is recorded in Schenker’s diary for February 21, 1931: From Oppel (letter): his lectures are now finished; to end with, he introduced Schenker the Philosopher of Art, which "ignited" [his audience]! Now, I certainly do not begrudge our dear, good Oppel the occasional extravagant locution, but even if used in error, a phrase such as this should not be allowed to slip in. Of course, he meant by it something more than a theorist or a musician; but he overlooked that, in doing so, he has raised Kurth to the level of – a musician, and reckons me as a philosopher as opposed to a musician. That he thereby says precisely the opposite of what I represent is something he does not seem to realize. Perhaps I shall bring up this problem of nomenclature some time in Galtür. The old leader writer for the Neue freie Presse, Dr. Goldbaum, expressed things much better in this respect when he said: "Call yourself a musician, after all; that is actually something more!" Now I am being deprived of being a musician and am turned into a philosopher, though I am the only true musician alive today! Oppel informs me that Peters would like to bring out a Chopin edition, prepared by Teichmüller; he has pointed Teichmüller in the direction of the [Photogramm] Archive but he doubts that he will take that path.”.

tei:div.tei:p: die Vorträge habe ich am Montag beendet, ich habe in den letzten über Ph E Bach, Haydn u. die Beethovens 1. Klaviersonate, 1. Satz, gesprochen. Und zuletzt habe ich den Damen den Kunstphilosophen Heinrich Schenker vorgeführt, und dieser Stoff hat am meisten gezündet. –

Leider habe ich schon bald 3 Wochen eine schwere Erkältung und bin manchmal stockheiser; ich fühle mich gar nicht wohl. All diese Spannungen: Berlin Kultusministerium, Wohnungssuche u. Umzugvorbereitungen sind regulärer Arbeit wenig günstig. –
In Kiel gehts gut. – Ich hoffe Sie beiden gesund bei der Arbeit.

Teichmüller hier,Robert Teichmüller (1863–1939) taught at the Leipzig Conservatory from 1897. He produced editions of Mozart’s sonatas and some piano works of Reger, but not of Chopin. College am Konserv., soll für Peters eine neue Chopinausgabe machen; ich habe ihm gesagt, das hätte nur einen Sinn, wenn es in Ihrem Sinne
resp. dem Chopins geschähe, mit genauester Durchsichtigung der Manuskripte. Ich verwies ihn auf Ihre Darstellung der Etuden es u. GesHeinrich Schenker, Chopin: Etude Es-Moll, op. 10, Nr. 6, Chopin: Etude op. 10 Nr. 5 (Ges-Dur), Das Meisterwerk in der Musik, vol. I (Munich: Drei Masken Verlag, 1925), 145–59, 161–73. im vorigen Jahrbuch. Nun bin ich gespannt, ob das was nutzt. –
Von den Kieler Studenten habe ich noch nichts wieder gehört, aber Ende Februar komme ich nach Hause u. werde mir den Führer citieren. –

tei:closer.tei:salute: Alles Gute und die herzlichsten Grüsse

tei:div.tei:p: I finished my lectures on Monday. In the last one, I spoke about C. P. E. Bach, Haydn, and Beethoven's first piano sonata, the first movement. And finally, I introduced the ladies to the art philosopher Heinrich Schenker, and this topic was the most engaging. –

Unfortunately, I've had a bad cold for almost three weeks now and am sometimes completely hoarse; I don't feel at all well. All these stresses: the Berlin Ministry of Culture, the apartment search, and the preparations for moving are not conducive to regular work. –
Things are going well in Kiel. – I hope you are both healthy and well.

Teichmüller, a colleague at the Conservatory here, is supposed to be preparing a new Chopin edition for Peters; I told him that it would make sense only if it were done in your spirit,
or rather, in Chopin's spirit, with the most meticulous examination of the manuscripts. I referred him to your analysis of the Etudes in E minor and G major in your previous Yearbook. Now I'm curious to see if that will be of any use. –
I haven't heard anything from the Kiel students yet, but I'll be coming home at the end of February and will contact the conductor.

tei:closer.tei:salute: All the best and most cordial greetings
